samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Converts parallel data to serial format.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal for serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="cnt" width_description="[2:0]">Counter for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="valid" width_description="[2:0]">Valid signal for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data" width_description="[3:0]">Data register for parallel data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module parallel2serial converts parallel data to serial format. The module has 4-bit parallel data input (d) and a 3-bit counter (cnt) register. The counter (cnt) is used to control the serial output. The counter (cnt) is incremented by 1 when the valid signal (valid) is set to 1. The valid signal (valid) is set to 1 when the counter (cnt) is equal to 3, which indicates that all 4 bits of the parallel data have been converted to serial format. The data register (data) stores the parallel data. The data register (data) is updated with the parallel data (d) when the counter (cnt) is equal to 3, and then shifted left by 1 bit for the next conversion.
    </implementation>
</module>
